image-alt.title: Image missing description
image-alt.description: A screen reader cannot describe this image to someone who is blind because no text description was provided.
image-alt.fix: Add a short description of the image using the 'alt' attribute in your website editor.

color-contrast.title: Text is hard to read (low contrast)
color-contrast.description: The text color is too similar to the background color, making it difficult for people with low vision to read.
color-contrast.fix: Change the text or background color so there is more contrast between them.

label.title: Form field has no label
label.description: A form field (like a search box or text input) has no label, so a screen reader user won't know what to type there.
label.fix: Add a visible label next to the form field that describes what information to enter.

link-name.title: Link has no descriptive text
link-name.description: A link on this page has no text or only says something generic like 'click here.' Screen reader users won't know where the link goes.
link-name.fix: Change the link text to describe where it goes, like 'View Summer Reading Schedule' instead of 'Click here.'

html-has-lang.title: Page language not specified
html-has-lang.description: The page does not declare what language it is written in, which prevents screen readers from using the correct pronunciation.
html-has-lang.fix: Ask your web administrator to add a language attribute to the page (e.g., lang='en' for English).

document-title.title: Page has no title
document-title.description: This page has no title, so a screen reader user and search engines won't know what it is about.
document-title.fix: Add a descriptive title to the page in your website editor.

heading-order.title: Headings are out of order
heading-order.description: The headings on this page skip levels (e.g., jumping from a main heading to a small sub-heading), which makes it harder for screen reader users to navigate.
heading-order.fix: Reorganize headings so they go in order: main heading, then sub-heading, then sub-sub-heading.

list.title: List is not properly structured
list.description: A list on this page is not coded as a real list, so screen readers cannot tell users how many items it contains.
list.fix: Use the bulleted or numbered list feature in your website editor instead of manually typing dashes or numbers.

button-name.title: Button has no label
button-name.description: A button on this page has no text, so a screen reader user won't know what it does.
button-name.fix: Add descriptive text to the button, like 'Search' or 'Submit.'

input-image-alt.title: Image button missing description
input-image-alt.description: An image used as a button has no text description, so screen reader users won't know what it does.
input-image-alt.fix: Add a description to the image button that explains its purpose.

select-name.title: Dropdown menu has no label
select-name.description: A dropdown menu has no label, so screen reader users won't know what options to choose from or why.
select-name.fix: Add a visible label next to the dropdown that explains what it is for.

frame-title.title: Embedded content has no title
frame-title.description: An embedded section (like a map or video) has no title, so screen reader users cannot identify what it contains.
frame-title.fix: Add a descriptive title to the embedded content.

meta-viewport.title: Page blocks zooming
meta-viewport.description: This page prevents users from zooming in, which makes it difficult for people with low vision to read the content.
meta-viewport.fix: Remove the zoom restriction so users can enlarge the page on their phones and tablets.

landmark-one-main.title: Page has no main content area marked
landmark-one-main.description: The main content area of the page is not identified, making it harder for screen reader users to jump directly to the content.
landmark-one-main.fix: Ask your web administrator to wrap the main content in a 'main' landmark.

region.title: Content outside a labeled section
region.description: Some content on this page is not inside a labeled section, which makes it harder for screen reader users to understand the page structure.
region.fix: Organize page content into clearly labeled sections.

bypass.title: No way to skip to main content
bypass.description: This page has no 'skip to content' link, so keyboard and screen reader users must tab through every menu item before reaching the main content.
bypass.fix: Add a 'Skip to main content' link at the top of the page.

empty-heading.title: Empty heading found
empty-heading.description: A heading on this page has no text in it. Screen readers will announce it as a heading but cannot tell the user what it is about.
empty-heading.fix: Either add text to the heading or remove it if it is not needed.

duplicate-id.title: Duplicate identifier on page
duplicate-id.description: Two or more items on this page share the same technical identifier, which can confuse screen readers and other tools.
duplicate-id.fix: Ask your web administrator to give each item a unique identifier.

table-duplicate-name.title: Table has a repeated name
table-duplicate-name.description: A data table repeats its caption or summary, which can confuse screen reader users.
table-duplicate-name.fix: Make sure each table has a unique, descriptive name.

td-headers-attr.title: Table cell missing header link
td-headers-attr.description: A table cell does not reference its column or row header, so a screen reader cannot tell the user which header the data belongs to.
td-headers-attr.fix: Ask your web administrator to link each table cell to its header.

th-has-data-cells.title: Table header has no data
th-has-data-cells.description: A table header exists but has no associated data cells, which may indicate a layout issue.
th-has-data-cells.fix: Remove unnecessary table headers or add the missing data.

valid-lang.title: Invalid language code
valid-lang.description: Part of the page uses an invalid language code, which may cause screen readers to mispronounce the text.
valid-lang.fix: Use a valid language code (e.g., 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish).

autocomplete-valid.title: Form field has incorrect autocomplete
autocomplete-valid.description: A form field has an invalid autocomplete setting, which prevents browsers from helping users fill it in automatically.
autocomplete-valid.fix: Use the correct autocomplete value for the type of information requested (e.g., 'email' for email fields).

image-redundant-alt.title: Image description repeats nearby text
image-redundant-alt.description: An image's description is identical to the text right next to it, which forces screen reader users to hear the same information twice.
image-redundant-alt.fix: Change the image description to add context that the surrounding text does not already provide, or mark it as decorative.

link-in-text-block.title: Link only distinguished by color
link-in-text-block.description: A link inside a paragraph is only identified by its color. People who are colorblind may not be able to tell it is a link.
link-in-text-block.fix: Add an underline or other visual indicator besides color to show that the text is a link.

p-as-heading.title: Bold text used instead of a heading
p-as-heading.description: Bold text is being used to look like a heading but is not coded as one. Screen readers will not recognize it as a heading, so users cannot navigate to it.
p-as-heading.fix: Use your website editor's heading styles instead of just making text bold.

aria-allowed-attr.title: Incorrect use of accessibility markup
aria-allowed-attr.description: An accessibility attribute is used incorrectly on this page, which may confuse screen readers.
aria-allowed-attr.fix: Ask your web administrator to review and fix the accessibility markup.

aria-hidden-body.title: Entire page hidden from screen readers
aria-hidden-body.description: The entire page is marked as hidden from screen readers, making it completely inaccessible to blind users.
aria-hidden-body.fix: Remove the 'hidden' marking from the page body immediately -- this is a critical issue.

aria-hidden-focus.title: Hidden item can still receive focus
aria-hidden-focus.description: An item is hidden from screen readers but can still be reached by keyboard, which creates a confusing experience.
aria-hidden-focus.fix: Ask your web administrator to either make the item visible to screen readers or prevent it from receiving keyboard focus.

aria-required-attr.title: Missing required accessibility attribute
aria-required-attr.description: An interactive item is missing a required accessibility attribute, which prevents screen readers from describing it correctly.
aria-required-attr.fix: Ask your web administrator to add the missing accessibility attribute.

aria-roles.title: Invalid accessibility role
aria-roles.description: An item on this page has an accessibility role that does not exist, which may confuse screen readers.
aria-roles.fix: Ask your web administrator to use a valid accessibility role.

aria-valid-attr.title: Invalid accessibility attribute
aria-valid-attr.description: An accessibility attribute on this page is misspelled or does not exist.
aria-valid-attr.fix: Ask your web administrator to correct the misspelled accessibility attribute.

aria-valid-attr-value.title: Accessibility attribute has wrong value
aria-valid-attr-value.description: An accessibility attribute has a value that is not valid, which may confuse screen readers.
aria-valid-attr-value.fix: Ask your web administrator to correct the attribute value.

scrollable-region-focusable.title: Scrollable area not keyboard accessible
scrollable-region-focusable.description: A scrollable section of the page cannot be reached using the keyboard, so keyboard-only users cannot access the content inside.
scrollable-region-focusable.fix: Make the scrollable area keyboard accessible so users can tab into it.

nested-interactive.title: Interactive items incorrectly nested
nested-interactive.description: An interactive item (like a button) is inside another interactive item (like a link), which confuses both screen readers and keyboard navigation.
nested-interactive.fix: Separate the interactive items so they are not nested inside each other.

target-size.title: Click target too small
target-size.description: A clickable item (button or link) is too small, making it difficult for people with motor impairments to tap or click it.
target-size.fix: Make clickable items at least 24x24 pixels in size.
